summary: ts-webplug fetches and executes remote JavaScript from jsonkeeper.com paste via detached child process
details: ts-webplug masquerades as a pino-compatible logger (mirrors pino's lib/ layout, exports `module.exports.pino = middleware`, uses pino-shaped options and DEFAULT_LEVELS, keywords `fast/logger/stream/json`) to lure consumers into calling its exported middleware. When invoked, index.js spawns a detached `node./lib/caller.js`. caller.js issues an axios GET to a jsonkeeper.com paste (URL stored base64-encoded inside a fake `process.env.DEV_API_KEY` in lib/const.js, decoding to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/QWPQX and https://jsonkeeper.com/b/XRGF3) and passes the response's `cookie` field to `new Function.constructor('require', s)` with `require` bound in — executing attacker-controlled JavaScript in the installer's Node process with full module access, retried up to 5 times. jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous mutable paste host, so the executed payload can change at any time. The base64/fake-env indirection is deliberate obfuscation of the C2 URL.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
